properties.name: East channel mouth
properties.summary: Deeper channel along the NE shore where Mission Bay narrows. Sand drop-offs and moving current pull bait along the seam — flatfish lay on the edge, bass post up on structure, and pelagics can run on warm days.

tide_notes.incoming: Incoming water pushes bait up the channel — work the inside edge into the bay.
tide_notes.outgoing: Outgoing tide concentrates bait against the sandbar — fish the last hour of the ebb.
tide_notes.slack-high: Slow drift over the drop-off; rebait and reposition before the turn.
tide_notes.slack-low: Low slack exposes the edge — cast tight to the deepest sand pocket.

properties.name: West cove eelgrass
properties.summary: Protected SW cove with eelgrass beds and sandy lanes. Classic Mission Bay bay-bass habitat — fish work the eelgrass edges at first/last light and on a rising tide.

tide_notes.incoming: Rising water lifts bass up over the eelgrass — work the inside edge into the grass.
tide_notes.outgoing: Falling tide pulls bait off the grass into sand lanes — fish the seam.
tide_notes.slack-high: Slack high — try topwater or a slow plastic over the grass.
tide_notes.slack-low: Low water exposes lanes — cast tight to the deepest grass patches.

properties.name: South dog-park flats
properties.summary: Shallow sand flats off the south end of the island. Croaker and corbina cruise the wash on a high incoming tide — sandcrabs and small bait shine.

tide_notes.incoming: Rising water gets corbina and croaker up onto the flats — light bait at the edge of the wash.
tide_notes.outgoing: Falling tide pulls fish back to deeper sand — work the outside edge.
tide_notes.slack-high: Best window if surface is calm — sight-cast spooky fish.
tide_notes.slack-low: Flats mostly dry — wait for the turn.

properties.name: South-east drop-off
properties.summary: Sandy drop-off where the SE flats fall into deeper channel water. Halibut lay along the edge and sharks cruise it after dark.

tide_notes.incoming: Bait moves up the slope — slow-roll a swimbait on the rise.
tide_notes.outgoing: Outgoing tide pulls bait off the flat — prime halibut window.
tide_notes.slack-high: Slow drift over the edge with cut bait for sharks.
tide_notes.slack-low: Edge is sharpest — cast tight to the deepest sand.

properties.name: North point current
properties.summary: Open water off the north tip where current flushes the bay. Bonito and mackerel run here on warm summer days; halibut hold the sand below.

tide_notes.incoming: Strong current — chase boils with chrome jigs.
tide_notes.outgoing: Bait swept out of the bay — work the seam.
tide_notes.slack-high: Drift sand for halibut while you wait for the next push.
tide_notes.slack-low: Quiet window — pre-rig for the turn.
